title: Agency in Language, Alane Suhr | Compile 26
summary: Alane Suhr discusses agency in language, examining the meanings we create with words and what language models actually are. She argues that agency resides with humans, not with the concept of AI itself.
writeup: Alane Suhr's Compile 26 talk argues that agency in human-AI language interactions resides with people, not with the language systems. The talk separates what language models technically are from how they tend to be conceptualized, and it treats instruction tuning and RLHF as the mechanisms that shape model behavior. The framing matters for how the field attributes intent and responsibility to models versus their users.
